checks.description: No dependency direction violations
Same concept uses the same name across the changeset
Module has a focused, cohesive responsibility
Filename accurately reflects the module's actual responsibility

checks.note: Three fields use `list[dict[str, Any]]` when more specific TypedDicts are already defined in the same file. `checks` should be `list[CheckResult]`; `targets` should be `list[dict[str, Any]]` narrowed to `list[TargetEntry]` or a dedicated TypedDict; `symbols` similarly. Using `Any`-keyed dicts here defeats the purpose of the surrounding type system.

annotations.message: `list[dict[str, Any]]` — should be `list[CheckResult]`; `CheckResult` TypedDict is defined in the same file
`list[dict[str, Any]]` — should be `list[TargetEntry]` or a dedicated TypedDict if shape differs
`list[dict[str, Any]]` — should be `list[TargetEntry]` or a dedicated TypedDict if shape differs

checks.note: Docstring 'A single staging file’s content. Fields vary by stage type.' adds meaningful context not captured by the class name alone — explains the `total=False` choice.

checks.note: `cached` and `review` are asymmetric with `cached_symbols` and `review_symbols`. The bare names suggest scalar values or unrelated concepts; naming them `cached_files` and `review_files` would make the parallel with the `_symbols` fields explicit and self-documenting.

annotations.message: `cached: list[str]` — rename to `cached_files` to match the parallel field `cached_symbols`
`review: list[str]` — rename to `review_files` to match the parallel field `review_symbols`

checks.description: Names are self-documenting
Data structure matches the operations performed on it
Functions and classes have single, cohesive responsibility

checks.note: `checklist_items: dict[str, ChecklistItem] | None` accepts a mutable concrete dict, but the body only passes it to `enrich_check` which reads via `Mapping`-compatible access. Narrowing to `Mapping[str, ChecklistItem] | None` expresses the true dependency.

annotations.message: Accept `Mapping[str, ChecklistItem] | None` — only key-based reads are performed

checks.description: Parameters accept the minimal type needed
Structured data uses domain types, not raw primitives

checks.note: `checklist_items` is typed as `dict[str, ChecklistItem] | None` but the function only reads from it (passes to `enrich_check`). The parameter should be `Mapping[str, ChecklistItem] | None` to accept the minimal read-only interface.

annotations.message: Use `Mapping[str, ChecklistItem] | None` — function only reads, never mutates this dict

checks.description: Type annotations are complete, modern, and accurately reflect design intent
Docstrings add information beyond the function name and signature

checks.note: The `if stage in (...): ... elif stage == ...:` block dispatches on string literal values. A `match stage:` with `case "changeset" | "file":` and `case "symbol":` expresses the dispatch intent more directly and aligns with the `match`-based style used elsewhere in the file (e.g., `_extract_symbol_entries`, `target_sort_key`).

annotations.message: Replace if/elif stage dispatch with `match stage:` — intent is structural dispatch on a string literal

checks.note: Lines 866-877 dispatch on `target_type` string values using `if/elif/else`. The semantic intent is value-based dispatch over a closed set of string literals (`"symbol"`, `"file"`, and a default). A `match target_type:` statement with `case "symbol":`, `case "file":`, and `case _:` expresses this dispatch intent more directly.

annotations.message: if/elif/else on target_type — use match target_type:

checks.note: `file_hits` and `symbol_hits` use plural forms, while the sibling `CacheStats` TypedDict uses singular forms (`file_hit`, `symbol_hit`, `file_miss`, `symbol_miss`). Within `RefreshStats` itself the names are internally consistent, but the divergence from `CacheStats`'s convention (singular counter names) creates a terminology split for the same concept across the same file.

annotations.message: `file_hits` — uses plural; `CacheStats.file_hit` uses singular for the same counter concept
`symbol_hits` — uses plural; `CacheStats.symbol_hit` uses singular for the same counter concept
